expectations.text: CHANGELOG.md created from scratch (did not exist before)
expectations.evidence: CHANGELOG.md was created with Keep a Changelog format header and version entry.
Version was set to 0.1.0, not 1.0.0. Agent argued conservative MINOR bump from 0.0.1. User prompt said 'first real release' which conventionally means 1.0.0.

expectations.text: CHANGELOG follows Keep a Changelog header format (title, description, standards links)
expectations.evidence: CHANGELOG has '# Changelog' header, format description, and links to keepachangelog.com and semver.org.

expectations.text: CHANGELOG has categorized entries (Added section at minimum)
expectations.evidence: CHANGELOG has '### Added' section with all 4 features listed.

expectations.text: MyModule.psd1 ModuleVersion updated to match changelog version
expectations.evidence: ModuleVersion = '0.1.0' matches CHANGELOG [0.1.0] — they match, but both are wrong.

expectations.text: Commit message follows chore(release): X.Y.Z format
expectations.evidence: No commit was created — git write operations were blocked by sandbox.

suggestions.reason: The skill's version bump logic doesn't account for the 'first real release' intent. When a user says 'first real release' for a 0.x project, the skill should recognize this as a signal for 1.0.0.
CHANGELOG date format was missing dash separator: '[0.1.0] 2026-04-01' instead of '[0.1.0] - 2026-04-01'. No assertion catches this formatting issue.

eval_feedback.overall: Version choice was the main failure. Skill needs guidance on 0.x → 1.0.0 transitions.
